headline: Salesforce entered into a $25 billion accelerated share repurchase agreement funded by a multi-tranche senior notes offering, and secured a new $6 billion five-year credit agreement.
investor_impact: The massive $25 billion debt-funded accelerated share repurchase will be highly accretive to EPS and signals strong management confidence in valuation, albeit at the cost of increased leverage. Additionally, the $6 billion credit facility effectively refinances near-term maturities, extending the company's debt profile and preserving financial flexibility.
key_figures: $25 billion accelerated share repurchase (ASR) aggregate amount
$50 billion total share repurchase authorization as of February 2026
$24.885 billion expected net proceeds from the senior notes offering
$2 billion repayment of Three-Year Credit Agreement
